description:
AJ Fernandez Bella Artes is a refined medium plus cigar built around a Rojita hybrid wrapper, a Nicaraguan binder, and a mix of Nicaraguan and Honduran fillers. The profile opens with cream, light spice, and a subtle floral note that sets a smooth tone from the start.
As it burns, the flavor develops into soft cinnamon, cocoa, and a gentle sweetness over a steady earthy core. The construction is dependable and the strength stays balanced, making Bella Artes a strong option for smokers who want depth and complexity without heavy power.

Flavor_Profile: Cedar, White Pepper, Cinnamon, Sweet Spices
Earth, Espresso, Dense array of sweet and spicy notes
description:
AJ Fernandez New World Oscuro is one of the blends that put AJ on the map as a modern powerhouse in cigar making. Developed with his father, Ismael Fernandez, this cigar was created to honor the history of tobacco in the Americas and became an instant hit when it launched. The red box has become a familiar sight in humidors, known for offering serious value without cutting corners on craftsmanship.
The blend is built around all Nicaraguan tobaccos, including a dark oscuro wrapper that gives the cigar its signature richness and depth. New World Oscuro is known for dependable construction and a bold profile that helped solidify AJ’s reputation for producing high quality Nicaraguan cigars at accessible prices. It remains one of the core lines that introduced many smokers to his style of blending.

description: This cigar features a dark Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro wrapper over a core of Dominican long filler and binder, offering a smooth experience with full flavor and a mild to medium body. As the Maduro version of one of the brand’s longest-running best sellers in our store, it stays true to Fuente’s signature balance and consistency. A reliable classic with just enough richness to keep seasoned smokers coming back.

This cigar has earned its reputation over the past 30 years as a shop staple and one of our top sellers. Wrapped in a toothy Cameroon leaf over Dominican binder and filler, it delivers a mild to medium body with rich, nutty flavors that show exactly why the blend has lasted this long. Familiar, reliable, and always in demand.

Once a limited run, now a regular in the Fuente lineup, the 8-5-8 Sun Grown offers a deeper take on a familiar favorite. The Ecuadorian sun grown wrapper adds a layer of spice and body to the Dominican core, bringing out notes of leather, cedar, cinnamon, and a warm, earthy finish. Medium-bodied and well-balanced, it adds just enough edge without losing its roots.

Originally created out of necessity after a storm wiped out Fuente’s wrapper supply, the Añejo has since earned its place as one of the family’s most celebrated releases. Built with the same aged Dominican filler and binder found in the Opus X line, the blend is finished with a Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro wrapper that spent time resting in cognac barrels. Full-bodied and complex, this cigar offers deep notes of cocoa, baking spice, and a subtle sweetness that sets it apart. Once rare, still revered.

Flavor_Profile: Dark spicy kick, Coffee, Sweetness, Vanilla
description: Crafted with a Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro wrapper over Dominican binder and filler, the Brevas Royale Maduro offers a medium-bodied profile with notes of coffee, sweetness, and vanilla. This cigar provides a consistent and approachable experience, suitable for both newcomers and seasoned enthusiasts. Its balanced flavor and accessible price point make it a reliable choice for everyday enjoyment.

Wrapped in Cameroon and filled with Dominican tobacco, this cigar leans mild to medium with notes of cedar, toast, and a touch of spice. Built for consistency, it’s an easygoing smoke that fits into just about any time of day. Affordable, dependable, and unmistakably Fuente.

Flavor_Profile: Cedar, Spice, Creamy finish, Natural Tobacco
description: This cigar stands as the final blend conceived by Carlos Fuente Sr., inspired by a dream and brought to life through his guidance from afar. Featuring an Ecuadorian Havana wrapper over Dominican binder and filler, it delivers a medium experience with notes of cedar, natural tobacco, and subtle sweetness. The Casa Cuba offers a balanced and nuanced profile, paying homage to traditional Cuban cigar-making techniques.

Flavor_Profile: Cedar, Spicy, Creamy Finish, Natural Tobacco
description: This cigar stands as the final blend conceived by Carlos Fuente Sr., inspired by a dream and brought to life through his guidance from afar. Featuring an Ecuadorian Havana wrapper over Dominican binder and filler, it delivers a medium bodied experience with notes of cedar, natural tobacco, and subtle sweetness. The Casa Cuba offers a balanced and nuanced profile, paying homage to traditional Cuban cigar-making techniques.

Crafted with an Ecuadorian Habano wrapper over Dominican binder and filler, this medium-bodied cigar offers a profile of cedar, baking spice, and a touch of citrus. The 6.5 x 42 Lonsdale format allows the wrapper's character to shine, delivering a balanced and nuanced experience. As a personal favorite of Ted's, this cigar exemplifies the harmony between size and flavor.

Handmade in the Dominican Republic, the Arturo Fuente Especiales line offers a medium-bodied profile with notes of cedar, oak, and subtle spice. Wrapped in an Ecuadorian Habano leaf over Dominican binder and filler, these cigars provide a consistent and approachable experience. Available in various sizes, including the Cazadores (6 x 50), Emperador (7 x 52), and Conquistadores (5.5 x 56), the Especiales line delivers quality craftsmanship at an accessible price point.

Flavor_Profile: Cedar, Leather, Cocoa, Coffee,  Hints of spice
description: Wrapped in Connecticut Broadleaf and filled with Dominican tobaccos, this cigar offers a rich, medium-bodied profile built on notes of cocoa, cedar, and dark roasted coffee. The Rothschild size delivers a smooth draw and a clean, even burn from start to finish. A dependable Maduro with just enough depth to keep things interesting, it remains a favorite for everyday smoking without ever feeling routine.

Wrapped in a smooth Connecticut Shade leaf, this cigar delivers a mild to medium profile centered on cedar, cream, and a light sweetness. The Dominican filler and binder keep the smoke balanced and easygoing, while the Rothschild size ensures a steady, even burn. Unpretentious and consistent, this cigar has long been a go-to for those who want something mellow but not boring.

Rolled in a Connecticut Shade wrapper over Dominican binder and filler, this torpedo-shaped cigar delivers a mild to medium profile with notes of cedar, cream, and a hint of spice. The tapered head offers a more concentrated draw, bringing out the nuance in the wrapper. A refined option in the Chateau line, suited for those who prefer a classic flavor profile in a more elegant format.

Wrapped in an Ecuadorian Sun Grown leaf over Dominican binder and filler, this cigar delivers a medium-bodied profile with notes of cedar, leather, and a hint of spice. The wrapper adds a touch of natural sweetness, enhancing the overall balance. Consistent construction and a smooth draw make it a reliable choice for those seeking a flavorful yet approachable smoke.

The Royal Salute Maduro comes wrapped in dark Connecticut Broadleaf over Dominican binder and filler, offering a medium-bodied profile with notes of cocoa, espresso, and a hint of spice. Bold in size but easy in character, it’s a natural fit for those who prefer a longer smoke without the intensity to match.

Wrapped in a dark Connecticut Broadleaf over Dominican binder and filler, this Churchill sized cigar offers a medium bodied profile with notes of dark chocolate, licorice, almonds, and wood. Its consistent construction and smooth draw make it a reliable choice for those seeking a rich and balanced smoking experience.

Wrapped in a Cameroon leaf over Dominican binder and filler, this Lonsdale sized cigar offers a mild profile with notes of cedar, cream, and subtle sweetness. Its approachable flavor and affordable price point make it a reliable choice for everyday enjoyment.

Created by Carlos Fuente Sr. as a personal blend, this cigar represents one of the most respected names in the Fuente family legacy. Featuring a toothy Cameroon wrapper over aged Dominican filler and binder, it offers a medium bodied profile with notes of cedar, cocoa, and sweet spice. The Don Carlos line is a tribute to tradition. Refined, patient, and built with decades of experience behind every leaf.

Cigar Name: Don Carlos Eye of the Bull
description: This compact 3½ x 55 cigar delivers a medium to full-bodied profile with notes of Spice, Cedar, White Pepper, and Coffee. The Cameroon wrapper, combined with Dominican binder and filler, offers a rich and balanced experience. A distinctive feature is the dark tobacco circle on the cap, resembling a bull's eye, adding a unique touch to this limited release.

Cigar Name: Don Carlos Eye of the Shark

description: Created by Carlos Fuente Sr. as a personal blend, this cigar represents one of the most respected names in the Fuente family legacy. Featuring a toothy Cameroon wrapper over aged Dominican filler and binder, it offers a medium bodied profile with notes of cedar, cocoa, and sweet spice. The Don Carlos line is a tribute to tradition. Refined, patient, and built with decades of experience behind every leaf.

Created as a tribute to Carlos Fuente Sr., this cigar was originally reserved for his personal use before becoming available in limited quantities. It features the signature Don Carlos blend, a Cameroon wrapper over Dominican tobaccos but with additional age and a deeper, more rounded profile. Medium to full in body, it offers a balanced mix of natural sweetness and gentle spice. A dignified cigar that reflects the legacy of the man it was made for.

The Maduro version of the Chateau line features a dark Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper over Dominican binder and filler, offering a medium-bodied profile with notes of cocoa, earth, and subtle sweetness. Each size in the line maintains the same steady flavor focus, with just enough variation in shape to suit any smoker’s preference. Familiar, approachable, and true to Fuente’s house style, this line remains a dependable go to for those who enjoy a softer take on Maduro.

Wrapped in a golden Connecticut Shade leaf over Dominican binder and filler, the Chateau Natural line delivers a mild to medium bodied profile with notes of cream, cedar, and a touch of sweetness. Whether in the Rothschild, Churchill, or larger Double Chateau format, the blend stays consistent, smooth, balanced, and unmistakably Fuente. A reliable choice for those who enjoy a classic Connecticut without sacrificing character.

Flavor_Profile: Cedar, Cinnamon, Leather, Hints of tobacco sweetness
description: Using an Ecuadorian Sun Grown wrapper over Dominican binder and filler, the Chateau Sun Grown line offers a medium bodied profile with notes of cedar, spice, and a warm natural sweetness. The slightly darker wrapper adds depth without overwhelming the blend’s core character. Across all sizes, it remains a balanced and flavorful option for those who want a bit more body from the Chateau series without stepping into full strength.

Handmade in the Dominican Republic, the Arturo Fuente Especiales line offers a medium-bodied profile with notes of cedar, oak, and subtle spice. Wrapped in an Ecuadorian Habano leaf over Dominican binder and filler, these cigars provide a consistent and approachable experience. Available in various sizes, including the Cazadores (6 x 50), Emperador (7 x 52), and Conquistadores (5.5 x 56), the Especiales line delivers quality craftsmanship at an accessible price point.

The Don Carlos Eye of the Bull had an unusual introduction. When Arturo Fuente debuted it at the 2022 PCA Convention, Carlos Fuente Jr. personally asked those who saw it not to discuss it publicly. The cigar was on display but unfinished, and the request for silence only heightened the anticipation. It returned the following year as the centerpiece of the Fuente booth at the 2023 PCA, available exclusively to retailers who placed orders on site. That kind of controlled release is characteristic of how the Fuente family manages their most significant work.
The cigar is a personal project of Ciro Cascella, executive president of Arturo Fuente International, who spent several years developing it. It uses the established Don Carlos blend, an aged Dominican binder and filler under an African Cameroon wrapper, but applies it to a 3.5 x 55 short robusto format. The most visually distinctive feature is the dark tobacco accent applied to the cap, which creates the literal bull's eye the name references. The compact size concentrates the blend and delivers more body than typical Don Carlos vitolas. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

Wrapped in a dark Connecticut Broadleaf over Dominican binder and filler, this cigar offers a medium bodied profile with notes of cocoa, earth, and subtle sweetness. Its approachable flavor and affordable price point make it a reliable choice for everyday enjoyment.

Using an Ecuadorian Sun Grown wrapper over Dominican binder and filler, the Chateau Sun Grown line offers a medium bodied profile with notes of cedar, spice, and a warm natural sweetness. The slightly darker wrapper adds depth without overwhelming the blend’s core character. Across all sizes, it remains a balanced and flavorful option for those who want a bit more body from the Chateau series without stepping into full strength.

description:
The Arturo Fuente Gran Reserva is the foundational line of the family brand, built on a recipe of aged Dominican binder and filler tobaccos that has been refined across four generations. Every leaf used in the Gran Reserva is drawn from a private reserve set aside exclusively for the line, aged in Spanish cedar vaults before rolling. The Fuente family oversees every step of production at their factory in Santiago, Dominican Republic.
The Canones is the largest vitola in the Gran Reserva lineup at 8.5 x 52, a Presidente format that demands a significant time commitment and rewards patience. The natural version is wrapped in an African Cameroon leaf, a wrapper variety grown in Central Africa from Sumatran seed stock introduced after World War II and cultivated by small farmers on plots averaging around an acre. Cameroon wrapper is known for its fine, slightly oily texture and the mild natural sweetness it contributes to the smoking experience. The Canones builds gradually in body across its considerable length, starting smooth and gaining complexity through the final third. Medium strength. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Arturo Fuente Gran Reserva is the foundational line of the family brand, built on four generations of accumulated knowledge about Dominican tobacco and how to age and roll it. The Cuban Corona is a classic long corona at 5.75 x 44, a proportion with deep roots in Havana tradition and one that reflects the Fuente family's own Cuban heritage. Founder Arturo Fuente emigrated from Cuba to Tampa in the early twentieth century and brought those blending sensibilities with him to the Dominican Republic.
The blend is aged Dominican binder and filler under an African Cameroon wrapper, the same construction that runs through the Gran Reserva line. Each cigar is aged in Spanish cedar vaults before release. The longer, narrower proportions of the Cuban Corona allow the Cameroon wrapper to express itself gradually across a cooler, more measured burn than the shorter vitolas in the line. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Arturo Fuente Gran Reserva is the foundational line of the family brand, built on four generations of accumulated knowledge about Dominican tobacco and how to age and roll it. The Rothschilde takes its name from the classic petite robusto shape, a short, wide cigar at 4.5 x 50 with origins in nineteenth century European smoking culture. The proportions concentrate the blend and deliver a full expression of the tobacco in a shorter smoke than the longer vitolas in the line.
The construction is the same aged Dominican binder and filler under African Cameroon wrapper that defines the Gran Reserva across all its sizes, aged in Spanish cedar vaults before release. The Cameroon wrapper contributes its characteristic mild natural sweetness and fine, toothy texture. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series has been in continuous production since Carlos Fuente Sr. introduced it in 1983, reviving Cuban perfecto shapes his father Arturo had worked with decades earlier. The Best Seller Maduro carries the same tapered perfecto proportions as the natural at 4.5 inches with a ring gauge running from 43 at the head to 55 at the belly, dressed in a dark Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper in place of the traditional Cameroon. The Broadleaf contributes additional body and a heavier character than the natural version. Released in limited quantities seasonally. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series has been in continuous production since Carlos Fuente Sr. introduced it in 1983, reviving Cuban perfecto shapes his father Arturo had worked with decades earlier. The Best Seller is a petite perfecto at 4.5 inches with a ring gauge that tapers from 43 at the head to 55 at the belly, and among the most consistently sought after proportions in the lineup. The name is not marketing language. It earned that designation through decades of sustained demand. The blend is aged Dominican binder and filler under an African Cameroon wrapper, aged at Chateau Fuente before release. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway Classic is the largest vitola in the standard Hemingway lineup at 7 x 47, a Churchill perfecto that demands a significant time commitment and rewards a patient cadence. The perfecto taper at both ends is one of the most demanding shapes in the roller's repertoire, and production is limited to a small number of torcedores at the Fuente factory in the Dominican Republic who have mastered the technique. The Hemingway series was among the first modern lines to reintroduce this classic Cuban shape to the American market at scale, and the Classic remains its most ambitious expression within the standard line. The blend is aged Dominican binder and filler under an African Cameroon wrapper, aged at Chateau Fuente before release. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway Sungrown swaps the traditional Cameroon wrapper for an Ecuadorian sun grown leaf grown from habano seed, sourced through Oliva Tobacco Company. Sun grown tobacco develops thicker, darker leaves than shade grown varieties due to direct sun exposure, and the Ecuadorian growing conditions produce a wrapper with a notably darker color and fuller character than the Cameroon it replaces. The aged Dominican binder and filler underneath remain the same as the rest of the Hemingway line.
The Classic is a Churchill perfecto at 7 x 46, the largest vitola in the standard Hemingway lineup. The Sungrown is released in limited quantities annually and runs somewhat fuller in character than the natural Cameroon version. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series was introduced in 1983 by Carlos Fuente Sr. as a tribute to the classic Cuban perfecto shapes his father Arturo had worked with decades earlier. The Short Story is the smallest vitola in the standard lineup at 4 x 49, and in many ways the most democratic entry point into the series. At under an hour it fits the pace of a workday break or a quick afternoon smoke without asking much of the clock. The petite proportions concentrate the aged Dominican binder and filler under an African Cameroon wrapper into a compact, front loaded smoke that delivers the full character of the Hemingway blend in a fraction of the time. Its approachability has made it one of the most consistently sought after cigars in the entire Fuente portfolio and one of the harder sizes to keep in stock. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series has been in continuous production since Carlos Fuente Sr. introduced it in 1983, built around the Cuban perfecto shapes his father Arturo worked with in the early days of the family business. The Signature is a Grand Corona perfecto at 6 x 46, one of the most balanced proportions in the lineup, wide enough to develop a full expression of the blend across a comfortable hour or more without demanding the time commitment of the Classic. The Maduro variant dresses the aged Dominican binder and filler in a dark Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper in place of the traditional Cameroon, contributing additional body and depth. Released in limited quantities seasonally alongside the other Hemingway Maduro sizes. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series draws its name and its spirit from Ernest Hemingway, whose economy of expression and preference for the essential over the ornamental has a natural parallel in a cigar built around a single well executed idea. The Signature is a Grand Corona perfecto at 6 x 46, sitting between the Short Story and the Classic in the standard lineup and representing the most balanced proportion in the series. Wide enough to develop fully across its length, compact enough to finish in a comfortable sitting. The blend is aged Dominican binder and filler under an African Cameroon wrapper, aged at Chateau Fuente before release. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series has been produced at Tabacalera A. Fuente y Cia in Santiago since 1983, when Carlos Fuente Sr. revived the Cuban perfecto shapes his father Arturo had worked with in the early twentieth century. The Signature Sungrown applies the Hemingway blend to the Grand Corona perfecto at 6 x 46, the middle proportion in the standard lineup, dressed in an Ecuadorian sun grown habano seed wrapper sourced through Oliva Tobacco Company. The sun grown leaf develops darker and fuller than the traditional Cameroon wrapper, contributing additional depth to the same aged Dominican binder and filler that runs through the entire line. Released in limited quantities annually. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series has been in continuous production since Carlos Fuente Sr. introduced it in 1983, reviving Cuban perfecto shapes his father Arturo had worked with decades earlier. The Work of Art is one of the more visually striking vitolas in the standard lineup, a short wide perfecto at 4.875 x 60 that tapers dramatically from a 60 ring gauge at the belly down to a narrow closed nipple at the foot and a tapered head. It carries the largest ring gauge in the standard Hemingway lineup in one of its shorter lengths, a proportion that concentrates the blend and makes for a deceptively full smoke in a relatively compact cigar. The blend is aged Dominican binder and filler under an African Cameroon wrapper, aged at Chateau Fuente before release. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Arturo Fuente Gran Reserva is the flagship line of the family brand, built on four generations of accumulated knowledge about Dominican tobacco and how to age and roll it. The Petit Corona is one of the slenderest proportions in the lineup at 5 x 38, a traditional small corona that delivers a quicker, more concentrated smoke than the larger vitolas. The narrow ring gauge keeps the burn focused and the experience compact, typically under thirty minutes. The blend is aged Dominican binder and filler under an African Cameroon wrapper, aged in Spanish cedar vaults before release. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Arturo Fuente Chateau Fuente series takes its name from Chateau de la Fuente, the family's private estate deep in the Dominican Republic where the tobacco is grown and aged before release. Every cigar in the Chateau line is wrapped in the signature cedar sleeve that has become one of the more recognizable presentation elements in premium Dominican cigar making.
The Queen B is a Belicoso at 5.5 x 52, with straight sides and a tapered pointed head that concentrates the draw and sharpens the character of the blend through the opening of the smoke. The wrapper is an Ecuadorian sun grown leaf over aged Dominican binder and filler from the Fuente family's private reserve. The Queen B is available exclusively in the sun grown wrapper, making it one of the more focused expressions in the Chateau lineup. Small batch production. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

Flavor_Profile: Sweet cedar, Light leather, Light mineral
description:
The Arturo Fuente Rosado Sungrown Magnum R line was introduced in 2009 as a deliberate departure from the direction the premium cigar market was moving. While most manufacturers at the time were competing to produce the strongest, fullest cigars possible, Carlos Fuente Jr. went the other direction, building something medium in strength but full in flavor. The wrapper is the key to that balance. The Rosado Sungrown is an Ecuadorian habano leaf drawn from the lower primings of the tobacco plant, the leaves that grow closer to the ground and receive less direct sun than those at the top of the stalk. Those lower leaves develop a distinctive reddish character and a more nuanced profile, and Fuente ages them for nearly a decade before they reach a cigar.
The Vitola 44 name causes some confusion. It does not refer to the ring gauge of the cigar, which is actually 47. The 44 denotes the number of cigars in the box, an unusual count that reflects the Fuente family's willingness to package cigars on their own terms. The cigar itself is a Corona Extra at 4.875 x 47, the slenderest proportion in the Magnum R lineup and the one that earned a 94 rating from Cigar Aficionado and top 25 honors in both 2012 and 2016. Aged Dominican binder and filler. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

Flavor_Profile: Sweet cedar, Light leather, Light mineral
description: The Rosado Sungrown Magnum R line was introduced in 2009 using Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper leaf drawn from the lower primings of the plant and aged for nearly a decade, a wrapper Carlos Fuente Jr. had been holding in reserve from earlier production. The Vitola 52 is a Robusto at 5 x 52, and unlike the Vitola 44, the number here does refer to the ring gauge. It is the original size of the line, one of four proportions released at launch and the one that established the Magnum R's reputation for delivering full flavor from a medium strength smoke. Aged Dominican binder and filler. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

Flavor_Profile: Sweet cedar, Light leather, Light mineral
description: The Rosado Sungrown Magnum R was built around Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper leaf drawn from the lower primings of the plant and aged for nearly a decade, producing a reddish, nuanced leaf that delivers full flavor without the strength of Fuente's more aggressive lines. The Vitola 54 is a Toro at 6.5 x 54, the largest proportion in the original lineup and the one that gives the aged wrapper the most room to develop across its length. The extra length and ring gauge allow the blend to build gradually in a way the smaller vitolas cannot, making it the most expansive expression of the Magnum R character. Aged Dominican binder and filler. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

Flavor_Profile: Sweet cedar, Light leather, Light mineral
description: The Rosado Sungrown Magnum R was built around aged Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper leaf drawn from the lower primings of the plant, producing a reddish, nuanced leaf that delivers full flavor at medium strength. The Vitola 56 measures 5.58 x 56, the widest ring gauge in the original lineup and one of the more substantial proportions in the series. The extra girth slows the burn and deepens the body relative to the slimmer vitolas, giving the aged wrapper and Dominican tobaccos more surface area to express themselves across a longer, more deliberate smoke. Aged Dominican binder and filler. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Arturo Fuente Chateau Fuente series takes its name from Chateau de la Fuente, the family's private estate in the Dominican Republic where the tobacco is grown and every cigar is aged before being wrapped in the signature cedar sleeve. The Royal Salute is the largest vitola in the Chateau lineup at 7.625 x 54, a Double Corona that demands a patient afternoon and rewards it in full. The natural version is dressed in a Connecticut Shade wrapper, the same thin, silky leaf grown under cloth tents in the Connecticut River Valley that has defined mild premium Dominican cigars for decades. The Shade wrapper contributes a lighter, creamier character than the Sungrown or Maduro variants in the same vitola. Aged Dominican binder and filler. Boxes of ten. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Arturo Fuente Chateau Fuente series takes its name from Chateau de la Fuente, the family's private estate in the Dominican Republic where the tobacco is grown and every cigar is aged before being wrapped in the signature cedar sleeve. The Royal Salute Sungrown is the same Double Corona at 7.625 x 54 as the natural, dressed instead in an Ecuadorian sun grown wrapper that contributes a darker, fuller character than the Connecticut Shade of the natural version. Grown under direct sun rather than shade cloth, the Ecuadorian leaf develops thicker, more complex leaf with a deeper color and additional body. Aged Dominican binder and filler. Boxes of ten. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Arturo Fuente Gran Reserva is the foundational line of the family brand, built on four generations of accumulated knowledge about Dominican tobacco and how to age and roll it. The Churchill Seleccion d'Oro is a 7.25 x 48, one of the more stately proportions in the Gran Reserva lineup and a size that gives the blend considerable room to develop across its length. The d'Oro designation refers to the golden Connecticut Shade wrapper, a thin, silky leaf grown under cloth tents in the Connecticut River Valley that produces a mild, creamy character notably lighter than the Cameroon wrapped versions in the same line. Aged Dominican binder and filler, aged in Spanish cedar vaults before release. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series has been in continuous production since Carlos Fuente Sr. introduced it in 1983, reviving Cuban perfecto shapes his father Arturo had worked with decades earlier. The Short Story Maduro carries the same compact petite perfecto proportions as the natural, with a ring gauge tapering from 42 at the head to 49 at the belly, dressed in a dark Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper in place of the traditional Cameroon. The Broadleaf contributes additional body and a heavier character than the natural version in the same approachable proportion. Released in limited quantities seasonally. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series has been in continuous production since Carlos Fuente Sr. introduced it in 1983, reviving Cuban perfecto shapes his father Arturo had worked with decades earlier. The Signature Maduro is a Grand Corona perfecto at 6 x 46, the middle proportion in the standard lineup, dressed in a dark Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper in place of the traditional Cameroon. The Broadleaf contributes additional body and a heavier character than the natural version in the same proportion, making it the fuller of the two Signature options. Released in limited quantities seasonally. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

The Hemingway series has been in continuous production since Carlos Fuente Sr. introduced it in 1983, reviving Cuban perfecto shapes his father Arturo had worked with decades earlier. The Untold Story occupies a unique position in the lineup. At 7.5 x 54 it sits between the Classic and the Masterpiece in length while carrying a larger ring gauge than either, and unlike every other standard Hemingway vitola it has never been released with a Cameroon wrapper. The Maduro is the only version that exists. The Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper over aged Dominican binder and filler gives this particular perfecto a heavier, richer character than the Cameroon wrapped siblings, and the size demands a serious time commitment. Released in limited quantities seasonally. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

This cigar features a Cameroon wrapper over Dominican binder and filler, offering a mild to medium bodied profile with notes of cedar, cream, and subtle sweetness. Its compact size makes it an ideal choice for those seeking a brief yet flavorful smoking experience. Consistent construction and a smooth draw make it a reliable option for everyday enjoyment.

Ashton Classic has been a benchmark in premium cigars since the early 1980s, created by Philadelphia tobacconist Robert Levin and produced by the Fuente family in the Dominican Republic. The blend was designed to set a standard for consistency and elegance at a time when the premium cigar market was still taking shape, and it quickly became one of the most recognized names in the industry.
For decades, Ashton Classic has held its place as a dependable choice for new and seasoned smokers alike, known for its careful construction and long running partnership between Levin and the Fuentes. It remains one of the flagship lines that helped define modern Dominican cigar making and continues to represent the traditional side of premium cigars.

Ashton was founded in 1985 by Robert Levin, a Philadelphia tobacconist who partnered with the Arturo Fuente family to produce his cigars at their factory in the Dominican Republic. That partnership has defined the brand since its founding and gives Ashton direct access to the Fuente family's tobacco reserves and production standards. The Aged Maduro is widely considered the most rewarding blend in the standard Ashton lineup, rated 92 by Cigar Aficionado.
The wrapper is a Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro that undergoes up to three years of natural fermentation before rolling, an unhurried process that produces a dark, oily leaf without the bitterness that rushes fermentation can introduce. The binder and filler are aged Dominican tobaccos. Despite the depth and darkness of the wrapper the overall profile runs noticeably mild, making it one of the more approachable maduro cigars available at its quality level. Handmade at the Fuente factory in the Dominican Republic.

The Ashton brand was founded in 1985 by Philadelphia tobacconist Robert Levin in partnership with the Arturo Fuente family, who produce all Ashton cigars at their factory in the Dominican Republic. The Cabinet Selection was Ashton's first line extension beyond the Classic, and the distinction between the two lines comes down primarily to time. Where the Classic uses quality Dominican tobaccos, the Cabinet Selection uses Dominican long filler tobaccos aged for five to seven years, and the Connecticut Shade wrapper is aged for an additional year beyond what the Classic receives. Six or more different Dominican leaf varieties go into the filler blend, giving the Cabinet more complexity than the simpler construction of the Classic.
The Connecticut Shade wrapper produces the pale, chestnut hued color the Cabinet is known for, a thin and silky leaf grown under cloth tents in the Connecticut River Valley. The overall profile runs mild to medium with a clean, smooth character that has made this one of the benchmark cigars in the accessible premium category for decades. Rated 90 and above by Cigar Aficionado across multiple vitolas. Handmade in the Dominican Republic.

Flavor_Profile: Rich graham cracker, Cracked black pepper
description:
The Ashton Estate Sun Grown was created in 2005 to commemorate the twentieth anniversary of the Ashton brand, a collaboration between Robert Levin and Carlos Fuente Jr. that set out to produce the most ambitious cigar in the Ashton portfolio. The result is a true puro, with Dominican wrapper, binder, and filler all sourced from a single origin.
The wrapper is what defines the ESG. It is a sun grown Dominican leaf grown exclusively on the Fuente family's Chateau de la Fuente estate and used on no other cigar. The upper primings of the plant are reserved for this blend, producing a thick, oily leaf with more body and complexity than leaves harvested lower on the stalk. Combined with the aged Dominican binder and filler from the Fuente family's private reserves, the ESG is a considerably fuller and more complex cigar than anything else in the standard Ashton lineup. Production is limited to approximately 50,000 cigars annually. Rated 94 by Cigar Aficionado and ranked in the Top 10 Cigars of the Year in both 2006 and 2014. Handmade at the Fuente factory in the Dominican Republic.

Flavor_Profile: Dark chocolate, Black cherry, Ground pepper
description:
When Carlos Fuente Jr. introduced the Fuente Fuente OpusX in 1995 it demonstrated that Dominican tobacco could produce a full bodied cigar capable of competing with anything in the world. Robert Levin and Fuente Jr. built on that foundation with the Ashton VSG, released in 1999 as one of the first deliberately full bodied cigars built around Dominican tobacco. VSG stands for Virgin Sun Grown, a reference to the Ecuadorian Sumatra seed wrapper that defines the blend.
The wrapper is grown at a private estate in Ecuador by the Oliva Tobacco Company, a leaf grower not to be confused with the Oliva cigar brand, and produced exclusively for the VSG. It is harvested from the upper primings of the plant, the leaves that receive the most direct sun and develop the most intensity. The Fuente family then ferments the leaf extensively before rolling, producing a dark, oily wrapper with a lustrous sheen. Beneath it are four to five year aged Dominican binder and filler tobaccos from the Fuente family's private reserves. Many of the VSG vitolas are box pressed. Rated 94 by Cigar Aficionado. Handmade at the Fuente factory in the Dominican Republic.

Flavor_Profile: Chocolate, Nuts, Citrus, Earth, Hay, Slight hint of cherry
Chocolate, Nuts, Citrus, Earth, Hay, Slight hint of cherry

description:
The sweetened cap was a Cuban tradition that largely faded after the revolution scattered the island's cigar makers. Baccarat kept it alive. The brand traces its name to a legal dispute: the original cigar was sold under the Carl Upmann label by American Tobacco Company, until a 1978 lawsuit with the Cuban H. Upmann brand forced a rename. Industry veteran Sal Fontana had been selling that cigar for years before eventually joining Camacho Cigars in Honduras, where he spent the rest of his career as the brand's self-described consigliere. In 1989 Julio Eiroa relaunched Baccarat under Caribe Imports with a fully Honduran blend. When Davidoff acquired Camacho and its Rancho Jamastran factory in 2008, Baccarat came with it.
The Natural is built on Cuban-seed long-leaf filler grown in the Jamastran Valley, bound with Mexican leaf, and finished in a Connecticut shade wrapper grown from Connecticut seed in Honduras. A glucose-sealed cap delivers a faint sweetness from the first draw, a holdover from the Cuban cigar tradition the brand has never abandoned. Mild in body, consistent across a wide range of sizes, and handmade at the Diadema Cigars de Honduras factory in Danlí.

The Bolivar name dates to 1901, honoring Simón Bolívar, the Venezuelan general who led independence movements across six South American nations in the early nineteenth century. The brand carried Cuban prestige through most of the twentieth century before the non-Cuban rights passed through several hands. Scandinavian Tobacco Group quietly relaunched it in 2020 under their Forged Cigar Company division, assigning the blend to industry veteran Estélo Padrón.
The Cofradia is built at the HATSA factory in Danlí, Honduras, combining Honduran and Nicaraguan long-leaf filler in a Connecticut Broadleaf binder, finished with an oily Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper. Medium to full in body. Handmade in Honduras.

The Bolivar name dates to 1901, honoring Simón Bolívar, the Venezuelan general who led independence movements across six South American nations in the early nineteenth century. The brand carried Cuban prestige through most of the twentieth century before the non-Cuban rights passed through several hands. Scandinavian Tobacco Group quietly relaunched it in 2020 under their Forged Cigar Company division, assigning the blend to industry veteran Estélo Padrón.
The Cofradia is built at the HATSA factory in Danlí, Honduras, combining Honduran and Nicaraguan long-leaf filler in a Connecticut Broadleaf binder, finished with an oily Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper. Medium to full in body. Handmade in Honduras.

The Cofradia Oscuro takes the same Central American core as the original Cofradia and flips the construction. Where the Cofradia wraps in Ecuadorian Sumatra and binds in Connecticut Broadleaf, the Oscuro reverses them: a dark Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper over an Ecuadorian Sumatra binder, with a filler blend drawing from Nicaragua, Honduras, and the Dominican Republic. The three-country filler adds breadth the original's two-country blend doesn't have. Released in 2024, also made at HATSA in Danlí, Honduras. Medium to medium-full in body. Handmade in Honduras.

Simón Bolívar was born in Caracas but spent his life in motion across a continent, leading independence campaigns that eventually freed what is now Colombia, Venezuela, Ecuador, Peru, Panama, and Bolivia from Spanish rule. In the early nineteenth century, Colombia and Ecuador formed a single republic under his presidency known as Gran Colombia, or the Gran Republica. Forged Cigar Company built the blend around that history, deliberately sourcing tobacco from Colombia and Ecuador alongside Brazil and Nicaragua to make the geography of the cigar part of the tribute.
The wrapper is an Ecuadorian Habano leaf over a Brazilian Arapiraca binder, with long-leaf filler from Colombia, Brazil, and Nicaragua. Released in 2023. Medium to full in body. Made at the HATSA factory in Danlí, Honduras.

Flavor_Profile: Leather, Coffee, Hint of green apple
Earth, Cocoa, Espresso bean, black tobacco, gentle spices
description:
Asylum Cigars launched in 2012 as a collaboration between Tom Lazuka, Kevin Baxter, and Christian Eiroa, built around a deliberate rejection of convention through outsized ring gauges, extended smoking times, and blends that made no apologies for their intensity. The 13 was one of three cigars that debuted the brand and remains its foundation.
It is a Nicaraguan puro throughout. Cuban-seed long-leaf filler is grown in the black volcanic soils of Nicaragua and finished in a dark, oily Nicaraguan Habano wrapper. The line runs from a 5 x 50 robusto up through sizes that reach 8 x 80, with the larger sizes delivering smoking times well past two hours. Rated 91 by Cigar Aficionado. Medium to full in body. Handmade in Nicaragua.

Every cigar in the Asylum portfolio up to this point had leaned into intensity. The Insidious Connecticut was Tom Lazuka's deliberate departure from that pattern, designed to extend the brand's reach without compromising the quality that had built its reputation. It was the first Asylum cigar to use a Connecticut wrapper and the first to carry a sweetened cap.
The wrapper is an Ecuadorian Connecticut shade leaf over a Honduran binder and all-Honduran long-leaf filler. The sweetened cap is subtle rather than pronounced, adding a faint finish rather than defining the smoke. Mild in body. Handmade in Honduras.

Flavor_Profile: Dark chocolate, Nuts, Spices, Coffee notes
description:
In Honduras and across much of Latin America, azabache is a piece of jet black coal fashioned into a charm and given to newborns to ward off misfortune. Christian Eiroa named this cigar after it, building the wrapper to match: a deep, oily Mexican San Andrés Maduro leaf that is among the darkest in his catalog.
The Azabache was introduced in 2016 as a TAA exclusive, available only through Tobacconists' Association of America member shops. Demand pushed it into regular production. The filler draws from Honduras, Nicaragua, and Peru, over a Honduran binder, finished in the San Andrés wrapper. Slightly box pressed. Made at the El Aladino factory in Danlí, Honduras.

Flavor_Profile: Notes of wood, Pepper, Espresso, Dark chocolate

description: Christian Eiroa built his reputation on full-bodied Honduran tobacco, and the CLE Connecticut is a deliberate departure from that pattern. The core is aged Honduran long-leaf filler with a Honduran binder, finished in an Ecuadorian Connecticut shade wrapper. The result is mild to medium in body but carries enough complexity to stand apart from the typical Connecticut offering. Rated 91 by Cigar Aficionado. Made at the El Aladino factory in Danlí, Honduras.

The Eiroa family has been growing Cuban-seed Corojo in the Jamastran Valley of Honduras for generations, and the CLE Corojo was among the first cigars Christian Eiroa released when he launched CLE Cigar Company in 2012. It is a Honduran puro throughout, built from high-priming Corojo wrapper leaf over a Honduran binder and filler grown on the same family farms. The 11/18 vitola in the lineup was named for his mother's birthday. Rated 90 by Cigar Aficionado. Made at the El Aladino factory in Danlí, Honduras.

The Schizo Maduro is the value entry in the Asylum portfolio, built to deliver genuine flavor at an everyday price. It is constructed as a Cuban sandwich, meaning the filler combines both long and short leaf, a method that allows Honduran trim from other Asylum production runs to be used efficiently without sacrificing smoke quality. The result is covered in a Mexican San Andrés Maduro wrapper over Honduran filler, made at the El Aladino factory in Danlí, Honduras. Sold in bundles of 20. Medium in body.

The First 20 Years line sits under the Eiroa name rather than CLE, though both come from Christian Eiroa's El Aladino factory in Danlí. Where the original First 20 Years used darker leaf from a special parcel of Honduran tobacco given to Christian by his father Julio on the occasion of his 20th year in the industry, the Colorado is defined by its wrapper, a Honduran Corojo leaf aged over five years to achieve the specific reddish brown shade the name describes. Eiroa has said the Colorado color from Authentic Honduran Corojo was something he spent his entire career working toward. The filler and binder are also Honduran Corojo from the Eiroa family farms. Box pressed. Medium to full in body. Made at the El Aladino factory in Danlí, Honduras.

Camacho built its name on Corojo tobacco, and the Connecticut keeps that identity intact where it counts. The wrapper is an Ecuadorian Connecticut shade leaf, but the binder is authentic Honduran Corojo, which gives the blend a spice and structure beneath the surface that most Connecticut offerings don't carry. The filler combines Honduran and Dominican Aleman Ligero long-leaf tobaccos. The result is the mildest cigar in the Camacho portfolio, but with considerably more complexity than the wrapper color would suggest. Rated 90 by Cigar Aficionado. Made at the Diadema Cigars de Honduras factory in Danlí, Honduras.

Flavor_Profile: Hints of German chocolate cake, licorice, orange peel
description:
Camacho was founded in 1961 by Simon Camacho, a Cuban exile who brought Authentic Corojo seeds with him when he left the island and began growing them in Central America. When the Eiroa family acquired the brand in 1995 and moved production to their farms in the Jamastran Valley of Honduras, they continued cultivating that same Corojo lineage, spending decades refining the seed in Honduran soil.
The Corojo is the cigar that came from that effort. It is a Honduran puro throughout. The wrapper is a fifth priming Corojo leaf, the uppermost leaves of the plant. The binder is Corojo Ligero and the filler draws from three primings of the same Authentic Corojo. Rated 93 by Cigar Aficionado. Made at the Diadema Cigars de Honduras factory in Danlí, Honduras.

Flavor_Profile: Pepper, Tones of floral, Citrus notes
description: The Ecuadorian Habano wrapper is the defining element of this blend. Grown in the nitrogen-rich volcanic soils of the Andes foothills from Havana seed, it is a leaf with a distinct texture, color, and character that sets it apart from most other wrapper varieties on the market. Camacho built the Ecuador around it, pairing the wrapper with a Brazilian Mata Fina binder and a filler combining high-priming Honduran leaf and Dominican Pelo de Oro Ligero. Rated 91 by Cigar Aficionado. Medium to full in body. Made at the Diadema Cigars de Honduras factory in Danlí, Honduras.

Flavor_Profile: Intense Cedar, Dried Nuts, Cacao and Cream
Sweet, Spicy, Oak, Pepper, Dried fruit, Cinnamon
description: The Nicaragua takes its name from the Nicaraguan leaf in the filler blend rather than from its country of production, which is Honduras. The filler combines Nicaraguan tobacco with Honduran and Dominican leaf, wrapped in a Honduran binder and finished in an Ecuadorian wrapper. Released in 2020 as a regular production addition to the Camacho core lineup. Medium to full in body. Made at the Diadema Cigars de Honduras factory in Danlí, Honduras.

Flavor_Profile: Chocolate, Dark Fruit, Earth, Espresso, Pepper
description:
The Triple Maduro originated with a conversation between Christian Eiroa and Sal Fontana, Camacho's longtime sales director and consigliere, who had the idea of building a cigar in which every component, wrapper, binder, and filler, was maduro tobacco. Eiroa's first response was that it couldn't be done. Fontana persisted. Eiroa and his father Julio went to work on it, and after more than 80 blending attempts, they got it right. Introduced in 2007, it was the first all-maduro cigar in the premium cigar industry.
The wrapper is a dark, oily Mexican San Andrés Maduro leaf over a Corojo and Honduran Maduro binder, with Maduro long-leaf filler drawing from Honduras, the Dominican Republic, and Brazil. Medium to full in body. Made at the Diadema Cigars de Honduras factory in Danlí, Honduras.

CAO announced the America on July 4, 2007, part of the brand's World series that builds each blend around tobaccos from a specific region. The patriotic framing extends to the construction: both wrapper leaves are American grown, a Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro and a Connecticut Shade, spiraled together in a pinstripe barber pole pattern around the cigar. The two leaves provide contrast in both color and character across the smoke. Beneath them sits a Brazilian binder and a filler built entirely from ligero leaf, drawing from Nicaragua, Italy, the Dominican Republic, and the United States. Rated 92 by Cigar Aficionado. Medium to full in body. Made in Honduras.

When CAO began searching for Brazilian wrapper tobacco at the tail end of the 1990s cigar boom, Brazilian leaf was not a priority for most growers and the quality was inconsistent. After five years of searching, they found what they were looking for in the Bahia region, a dark, oily Arapiraca wrapper that became the foundation of the Brazilia. Introduced in 2001, it was the first mass-produced cigar to use a Brazilian Arapiraca wrapper and is widely credited with sparking renewed industry interest in Brazilian leaf, as other manufacturers followed CAO into the Bahia region in the years that followed.
The blend pairs that Arapiraca wrapper with a Nicaraguan binder and aged Nicaraguan long-leaf filler. Rated 91 by Cigar Aficionado. Full in body. Made in Honduras.

CAO has a pattern of building blends around doubling or tripling a single tobacco type, and the BX3 follows that logic to its natural conclusion. The name stands for Brazil times three. The wrapper is a Brazilian Mata Fina leaf, dark and oily with a Colorado hue. The binder is Brazilian Arapiraca, the same leaf that anchors the Brazilia line. The filler adds a third application of Brazilian Mata Fina alongside Honduran, Mexican, and Nicaraguan leaf to round out the blend. Available in three sizes, in 20-count boxes. Medium to full in body. Made at the STG Estelí factory in Nicaragua.

FASA takes its name from the Spanish word for phase, and the concept behind the line is as transparent as the name. CAO built two cigars around an identical core, a Cameroon binder and filler drawing from Dominican Piloto Cubano and three Nicaraguan growing regions, Jalapa, Estelí, and Condega, then covered each with a different wrapper to let the leaf do the talking. The Noche is the night entry, finished in a dark, rough Connecticut Broadleaf grown in the Connecticut River Valley. The Sol uses a Honduran Olancho San Agustín wrapper and is made at a different factory entirely, so the two cigars serve as a side-by-side study in how much the outermost leaf shapes the experience. Debuted at the 2024 PCA Convention. Available in two sizes in 24-count boxes. Medium to full in body. Made at the STG Estelí factory in Nicaragua.

The Sol is the day entry in the FASA pair, sharing the same Cameroon binder and filler of Dominican Piloto Cubano and Nicaraguan leaf from Jalapa, Estelí, and Condega as the Noche. Where the Noche goes dark with a Connecticut Broadleaf, the Sol is finished in a Honduran Olancho San Agustín wrapper, a leaf grown in the San Agustín Valley of Honduras. The two cigars are made in different factories and in different countries, which means the construction environment itself is also a variable alongside the wrapper. Debuted at the 2024 PCA Convention. Available in two sizes in 24-count boxes. Medium to full in body. Made at the STG Danlí factory in Honduras.

The Flathead debuted at the 2013 IPCPR trade show as a creation of Rick Rodriguez and Ed McKenna at General Cigar, built around American hot rod culture. The cigars are completely box pressed with a flat squared-off head, and each size is named after a car part. The box is designed to mimic an engine block and includes a vintage pinup print, a packaging concept that goes well beyond marketing gimmick given the cigar inside.
The blend starts with a dark, oily Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro wrapper over a Cuban-seed Connecticut Habano binder, with Nicaraguan and Dominican long-leaf filler at the core. Rated 95 by Cigar Aficionado and ranked number 3 Cigar of the Year in 2015, the highest rating CA has ever given a 60-ring gauge cigar. Full in body. Made in Nicaragua.

Flavor_Profile: Creamy, Ripe apple notes, molasses, brown sugar
description:
Italy has been growing Cuban Habano seed tobacco in the Benevento region, in the southern part of the country between Rome and Naples, for more than fifty years. Before CAO Italia, that leaf was used almost exclusively in machine-made cigars. Premium yields from Italian soil were too limited and inconsistent to justify hand-rolling. CAO sourced enough to build a filler blend around it, pairing the Italian leaf with Nicaraguan and Peruvian tobacco, and the result was one of the only premium handmade cigars in the world to use Italian-grown filler as a primary component.
The wrapper and binder are both Habano-seed leaf grown in Honduras. Rated 92 by Cigar Aficionado. Medium to full in body. Made in Nicaragua.

CAO was founded in 1968 by Cano Ozgener as a meerschaum pipe company in Nashville. When the brand expanded into premium cigars in the mid-1990s, it was the L'Anniversaire line, released in 1998 to mark the company's 30th anniversary, that put CAO on the cigar map. The Cameroon and Maduro versions debuted together and together they established the brand's reputation for quality handmade construction.
The Cameroon is a box pressed Nicaraguan puro from binder to filler, finished in a reddish-brown West African Cameroon wrapper. Rated 92 by Cigar Aficionado. Mild to medium in body. Made in Nicaragua.

The Mortal Coil is the first entry in CAO's Arcana series, a line built around rare and forgotten tobacco techniques rather than unusual growing regions. The defining tobacco here is andullo, a preparation method native to the Dominican Republic that dates back centuries. After curing, the leaves are tightly coiled and packed into large palm tree pods called yaguas, then wrapped in rope for additional compression while they ferment for up to two years. The result is a dense, concentrated log of tobacco that is typically chewed or smoked in pipes in the Dominican Republic, rarely appearing in premium handmade cigars.
CAO combined that andullo with Dominican Piloto Cubano, Nicaraguan Estelí, and Honduran Jamastran leaf in the filler, bound in a Connecticut shade binder and finished in a Connecticut Broadleaf wrapper. Originally released in 2021 as a limited run of 10,000 cigars, it returned as an annual limited release beginning in 2023. Available in a single size, 6 1/8 x 50 toro extra, in boxes of 20. Medium to full in body. Made at the STG Estelí factory in Nicaragua.

MX2 stands for Maduro times two, and the name is the entire concept. CAO introduced the blend in 2003 as part of what became an informal series of cigars built around doubling a single leaf type, the same logic later applied to BX3. Rather than simply selecting a dark wrapper, the blend layers two distinct maduro leaves: a Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro on the outside and a spicy Brazilian Maduro used as the binder. The filler draws from Nicaragua, Honduras, the Dominican Republic, and Peru. Medium to full in body. Made in Nicaragua.

The name Pilón Añejo describes two separate processes applied to the tobacco before you ever light it. A pilón is a large pile of leaf stacked for fermentation, in which the combined weight of the tobacco generates heat and moisture that cures the leaf. The circular pilón method is an older Cuban technique CAO revived in 2015 for the original CAO Pilón, having been largely abandoned over the previous century due to its cost and complexity. The Añejo takes that process one step further by aging the finished cigars for two additional years after rolling, añejo being the Spanish word for aged.
The blend uses an Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper, a Honduran Habano binder, and long-leaf filler from the Dominican Republic and Nicaragua. Blended by Rick Rodriguez. Limited to 5,000 boxes of 20. Medium to full in body. Made at the HATSA factory in Danlí, Honduras.

The Bragança tobacco at the center of this blend is grown on virgin rainforest land in a remote region of the Brazilian Amazon, harvested once every three years from the same plots. After harvest the leaves are hand-rolled into tight tubes called carottes and left to ferment under high pressure for six months. They then travel out of the rainforest by canoe before reaching the factory. The scarcity of the leaf is what makes the Amazon Basin a limited production release, available only when new Bragança tobacco comes in.
Blended by Rick Rodriguez and debuted in 2014, the cigar pairs that Brazilian filler with Colombian and Dominican Republic leaf, bound in a Nicaraguan binder and finished in an Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper. In place of a paper band, a length of twisted Bragança tobacco stem is coiled around the cigar. Available in a single size, 6 x 52 toro, in boxes of 18. Medium to full in body. Made in Nicaragua.

The non-Cuban Cohiba brand is owned by General Cigar, and the Blue was developed as a more accessible entry point in that portfolio. The defining component is the wrapper and binder leaf: a proprietary Honduran Olancho San Agustín varietal, grown in the volcanic soils of the OSA Valley in a collaboration with the Plasencia family. The same leaf debuted on the Partagas Heritage, but the Blue uses it on both wrapper and binder, giving the OSA tobacco more presence in the overall blend than any other cigar in the General Cigar lineup at the time of its release. The filler combines Honduran Jamastran, Nicaraguan Ometepe, and Dominican Piloto Cubano. Medium in body. Made at General Cigar Dominicana in the Dominican Republic.

The non-Cuban Cohiba brand has been owned by General Cigar in the United States since 1981, and the Red Dot is its flagship blend. The name comes from the red dot in the letter O on the band, a detail that became explicit when the packaging was updated in 2020 to print the words Red Dot directly beneath the Cohiba logo. The blend was the product of five years of development by General Cigar before its release.
The wrapper is an African Cameroon leaf, toothy and naturally sweet, over an Indonesian Jember binder and aged Dominican Piloto Cubano long-leaf filler. Rated 91 and 92 by Cigar Aficionado. Medium in body. Made at General Cigar Dominicana in the Dominican Republic.

The Riviera arrived in 2023 as the first new regular production Cohiba from General Cigar since 2020, and it broke with tradition in several ways at once. It is the first Cohiba to use a Mexican San Andrés wrapper, the first box-pressed Cohiba, the first with no Dominican tobacco in the blend at all, and the first to be made in Nicaragua rather than at the Dominican factory where nearly all General Cohiba cigars are produced.
The wrapper is a Mexican San Andrés Tuxtla leaf over a Honduran Connecticut binder, with a four region filler drawing from Honduran Jamastran, Honduran La Entrada, Nicaraguan Condega, and Nicaraguan Estelí. Ranked number 20 on Cigar Aficionado's Top 25 for 2024. Medium to full in body. Made at the STG Estelí factory in Nicaragua.

Flavor_Profile: Toasty undertones, Bold chocolate notes, Red wine
description:
Rolando Reyes Sr. founded Cuba Aliados in Havana in 1945, built it into one of the most recognized names in premium cigars, and lost his factory to the Castro government in the early 1960s. He spent the next several decades chasing the same terroir across New Jersey, Miami, the Dominican Republic, and eventually Honduras, where the brand remained until his death in 2012. Oliva acquired the trademark in 2021 and made an intentional choice in deciding who would relaunch it: Ernesto Perez Carrillo, who first crossed paths with Reyes in Miami decades earlier, and Julio Eiroa, from whom Reyes sourced tobacco during the Honduras years. The name Aliados, Spanish for allies, made the casting feel less like marketing and more like biography.
The Cabinet Edition is Carrillo's contribution, a limited release produced at his Tabacalera La Alianza factory in the Dominican Republic. The blend uses an Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper aged five years, a Nicaraguan binder, and long-leaf filler from the Dominican Republic and Nicaragua. Five sizes, 20-count boxes. Medium in body. Made at Tabacalera La Alianza, Dominican Republic.

The Cuba Aliados Original Blend is the everyday production line that Oliva commissioned when they acquired the brand in 2021, and the choice of factory was not arbitrary. Julio Eiroa of JRE Tobacco was the man Rolando Reyes Sr. sourced leaf from during the Honduras years, making him about as close to a natural custodian of the blend as anyone still working. Oliva's stated aim was to recreate the character of the cigars Reyes made in Cuba, which is why the blend is a Honduran puro despite the fact that Reyes himself famously had no use for Honduran tobacco for most of his career.
Wrapper, binder, and filler all come from Honduran tobacco grown in the Jamastran Valley at the Eiroa family farms. Five sizes, 20-count boxes. Medium to full in body. Made at the Fabrica de Puros Aladino S.A. factory in Danlí, Honduras.

Flavor_Profile: Cream, Woody and sweetness notes, Peppery finish

description:
The Disciple started as Diesel's first TAA exclusive in 2021, a collaboration between A.J. Fernandez and Justin Andrews, then Diesel's brand manager. It was also Diesel's first lancero since STG repositioned the brand as a brick and mortar line, and the TAA placement was a deliberate statement about that repositioning. The cigar landed well enough that it moved into wider release and eventually expanded to additional sizes beyond the original 7x38 lancero.
The blend uses a Mexican San Andrés wrapper over an Ecuadorian Sumatra binder, with Nicaraguan Habano filler from Estelí throughout. Medium to full in body. Made at Tabacalera A.J. Fernandez in Estelí, Nicaragua.

Flavor_Profile: Robust earth, Wood, Notes of chocolate and pepper
description: The Estelí Puro is Diesel's first region-specific blend, introduced in September 2020. The premise is straightforward and the execution earns some credit for following through on it: every component comes from A.J. Fernandez's own farms in the Estelí region, which also happens to be where the cigar is rolled. The wrapper is Nicaraguan Habano from the La Lilia farm, named for Fernandez's grandmother. The binder comes from the San José farm, and the filler draws from multiple plots across Estelí including the San Lotano farm. A Nicaraguan puro in the most literal sense, grown and made within a few miles of itself. Three sizes, 20-count boxes. Medium to full in body. Made at Tabacalera A.J. Fernandez in Estelí, Nicaragua.

The concept behind the Vintage Maduro is straightforward: take the Diesel formula of bold Nicaraguan tobacco and build it around aged leaf. The filler is Nicaraguan Habano from Jalapa, aged between five and eight years before rolling, which gives the blend a depth that typically costs more to achieve. The binder is Nicaraguan Habano from Estelí, and the wrapper is Mexican San Andrés. A collaboration between A.J. Fernandez and Justin Andrews, as with the rest of the Diesel line. Five sizes, 20-count boxes. Medium to full in body. Made at Tabacalera A.J. Fernandez in Estelí, Nicaragua.

The Vintage Natural arrived in early 2024 as a companion to the Vintage Maduro, built around the same premise: Nicaraguan Habano filler from Jalapa, aged five to seven years before rolling. Where the Maduro puts a San Andrés wrapper over that leaf, the Natural uses an Ecuadorian wrapper, which lets the aged tobacco speak more directly. The binder is Nicaraguan Habano from Estelí throughout. A collaboration between A.J. Fernandez and Justin Andrews. Medium to full in body. Made at Tabacalera A.J. Fernandez in Estelí, Nicaragua.

Flavor_Profile: Strong oaky flavor, Hints of bourbon
description:
The Diesel Whiskey Row launched in June 2018 as the second brick and mortar Diesel release after the brand moved from catalog to retail the year prior. The concept came from a partnership with Rabbit Hole Distillery out of Louisville: spent bourbon barrels were shipped to A.J. Fernandez's factory in Estelí in 2016, and the Mexican San Andrés binder leaf was aged inside them using a process Fernandez developed specifically for the project. The barrel aging adds character to the blend without turning the cigar into an infused product.
The wrapper is Ecuadorian Habano, aged five years. The binder is Mexican San Andrés, bourbon barrel aged. The filler is a three-region Nicaraguan blend from Ometepe, Jalapa, and Condega, aged five to eight years. Four sizes, 25-count boxes. Medium to full in body. Made at Tabacalera A.J. Fernandez in Estelí, Nicaragua.

The Sherry Cask arrived as a follow-up to the Whiskey Row bourbon, and the barrel lineage behind it is a bit more traveled. The casks originally held 30-year-old Pedro Ximénez Sherry, were then sent to Rabbit Hole Distillery to produce their PX Sherry Finish Bourbon, and then shipped on to Tabacalera A.J. Fernandez in Estelí. The Brazilian Arapiraca binder was aged in those casks, making it the third life for the same wood.
The wrapper is Connecticut Broadleaf and the filler is Nicaraguan, with the barrel-aged Arapiraca binder doing most of the distinguishing work. Like the bourbon version, the barrel influence reads as character rather than infusion. Medium in body. Made at Tabacalera A.J. Fernandez in Estelí, Nicaragua.

The Worthy Fool is the third and final entry in the Fool's Errand trilogy, an annual limited series from Diesel that pairs each release with a collectible tarot card and an interactive game. The first entry was the Stubborn Fool in 2022, a Connecticut Broadleaf wrapped perfecto with Honduran components. The Simple Fool followed with an Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper over an all-Nicaraguan Habano blend. The Worthy Fool closes the trilogy on familiar ground: Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper, Nicaraguan Habano binder, Nicaraguan Habano filler, all in the same 5x58 perfecto used throughout the series. Boxes of ten, each with a collectible card. Full in body. Made at Tabacalera A.J. Fernandez in Estelí, Nicaragua.

ACID launched in 1998 when Jonathan Drew and Marvin Samel set up shop in Estelí and started curing tobacco in rooms lined with over 150 essential oils, botanicals, and herbs. The process, which Drew Estate calls the cuarto aromático method, is what separates the line from other infused products: the tobacco absorbs the aromatic environment over months of aging rather than being sprayed or dipped. The Blondie is the most widely recognized cigar in the portfolio and one of the bestselling infused cigars in the country.
The blend uses a Connecticut shade wrapper over Nicaraguan binder and filler, in a 4x38 petit corona with a pigtail cap and a sweetened cap seal. It smokes sweet, aromatic, and mild, with the infusion reading as floral and herbal rather than candy. Mild in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

Where the Blondie is compact and quick, the Extraordinary Larry goes the other direction entirely. At 6x60 it is one of the largest cigars in the ACID lineup, wrapped in a dark Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro over Nicaraguan binder and filler, and put through an additional round of infusion beyond the standard cuarto aromático process. The result is richer and heavier than most ACID expressions, with the sweet and aromatic character of the line amplified by both the Broadleaf wrapper and the extra infusion cycle. Medium to full in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

The Cold Infusion is among the most popular cigars in the ACID lineup and one of the more distinctive expressions in the line. Where most ACID blends lean sweet and floral, the Cold Infusion pulls toward tea and herbs, lighter and more refreshing than the heavier aromatic profiles elsewhere in the portfolio. The name refers to the specific infusion method used, which preserves more delicate botanical notes than the standard cuarto aromático process.
The blend uses a Connecticut shade wrapper over Nicaraguan binder and filler, in a 6.75x44 lonsdale. Mild in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

The Kuba Grande is the gordo expression of the Kuba Kuba blend, scaled up to 6x60 from the original 5x54 robusto. The blend is the same throughout the Kuba line: Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper over Nicaraguan binder and filler, infused in the cuarto aromático rooms with Drew Estate's botanical and essential oil blend. The Grande delivers a longer, slower smoke than the original while keeping the same aromatic character. Mild to medium in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

The Kuba Kuba is the flagship of the ACID line and one of the bestselling infused cigars in the country. It is the cigar that established the brand's identity when Drew Estate launched in 1998 and remains the reference point for everything else in the portfolio. The blend uses an Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper over Nicaraguan binder and filler, infused over months in the cuarto aromático rooms with Drew Estate's botanical and essential oil blend. At 5x54 it is compact enough for an hour's smoke while delivering the full aromatic character the line is known for. Mild to medium in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

The C-Notes are the smallest handmade cigar in the ACID lineup, a 3.75x22 cigarillo that delivers the full cuarto aromático infusion in a ten to fifteen minute smoke. The name is slang for a hundred dollar bill, which rolled up approximates the size of the cigar. Same construction as the rest of the line: Ecuadorian Sumatra wrapper over Nicaraguan binder and filler, infused with Drew Estate's botanical and essential oil blend. Mild to medium in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

Flavor_Profile: NOT infused, naturally sweet, easy on the nose
description:
Deadwood Tobacco Co. takes its name and spirit from the old mining town in the Black Hills of South Dakota, and each cigar in the line is named for one of its fictional inhabitants. Crazy Alice is the most recognized of the bunch, a short piramide with a pigtail cap that makes the shape immediately identifiable on the shelf.
Unlike the ACID line, Crazy Alice is not infused. The natural sweetness in the smoke comes from the tobacco itself, primarily the dark oily Nicaraguan Maduro wrapper over Nicaraguan binder and filler. The result is aromatic in character without the botanical overlay of the ACID products. At 4.5x52, it runs about forty-five minutes. Medium in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

Where Crazy Alice uses a Nicaraguan Maduro wrapper, Fat Bottom Betty puts a Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro over Nicaraguan binder and filler, which gives it a darker, earthier character with the natural sweetness the Broadleaf leaf is known for. Like Crazy Alice, it is not infused, and the sweetness comes from the tobacco rather than the botanical rooms. The line comes in robusto, toro, and gordito, with a sweetened cap seal across all sizes. Medium in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

The Leather Rose is the fourth and spiciest entry in the Deadwood lineup, added in 2020. Where Crazy Alice and Fat Bottom Betty both use Nicaraguan-forward or Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro builds, the Leather Rose goes in a different direction: a dark oily Brazilian Maduro wrapper over an Indonesian binder and Nicaraguan filler, a three-country combination that sets it apart from its sisters. Not infused, like the rest of the Deadwood line, with a sweetened cap. Available in a petit corona and a torpedo. Medium in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

Sweet Jane is the original Deadwood cigar, the one that started the line as a collaboration between Drew Estate and Vaughn Boyd's Deadwood Tobacco Company in South Dakota before it went national. Like the rest of the line it is not infused, with natural sweetness coming from the tobacco and a sweetened cap seal. Drew Estate has kept the binder and filler blend undisclosed, describing it only as aromatic and exotic Nicaraguan tobacco with extra fermentation that pulls the character toward pipe tobacco. The wrapper is Connecticut Broadleaf Maduro over Nicaraguan binder and filler. Available in a corona gorda and a Baby Jane cigarillo tin. Medium in body. Made at La Gran Fabrica Drew Estate in Estelí, Nicaragua.

description:
El Rey del Mundo is one of the oldest names in premium cigars, tracing its roots back to Cuba in the 1880s and later finding new life in Honduras after the revolution. The modern Honduran production carries forward that legacy, maintaining the brand’s connection to traditional craftsmanship and long-aged tobacco. For decades, El Rey del Mundo has represented the old school style of cigar making, built on patience, consistency, and genuine flavor.
The Natural version highlights Honduran and Nicaraguan tobaccos wrapped in a smooth Ecuadorian Sumatra leaf. The result is a cigar that captures the essence of the brand’s classic approach, offering a refined balance of wood, spice, and soft sweetness. El Rey del Mundo Natural remains a favorite among traditionalists who value heritage, honest construction, and the timeless art of slow, careful blending.

description: Built to celebrate a decade in the game, the 10 Años is everything Esteban Carreras does well. Bold, smooth, and no wasted motion. Think rich, steady flavor without the usual flash. No fancy box needed, just a cigar that proves ten years wasn’t wasted.

description:
JC Newman The American is one of the most meaningful projects in modern cigar making, built as a tribute to the history of the Newman family and the early days of the US cigar industry. Produced entirely in the historic El Reloj factory in Tampa, the cigar was created to revive the tradition of a fully American made premium cigar at a time when that idea had almost disappeared. Every component comes from the United States, from the tobaccos to the boxes to the bands, making it a direct link to the era when Tampa was the heart of American cigar production.
The blend features a Florida Sun Grown wrapper, a Connecticut Broadleaf binder, and filler grown in Connecticut and Pennsylvania. This structure showcases the range and character of American tobaccos while honoring the craftsmanship that kept the Newman company alive for more than a century. The American has earned a place as a flagship product not because of strength or trend chasing, but because it preserves a piece of cigar history that few companies have the ability or the will to recreate today.

description:
Oliva Series O is one of the cornerstone blends that helped establish Oliva as a major name in Nicaraguan cigars. Built on a traditional Cuban seed concept, the line was created to highlight classic Nicaraguan tobaccos and the family’s long history of growing and blending. For many smokers, Series O is the cigar that introduced them to Oliva and showed what the brand could do with straightforward craftsmanship and honest, well aged leaf.
The cigar is known for dependable construction, a steady burn, and the kind of balance that keeps it in regular rotation for both new and seasoned smokers. Series O has been on shelves for years and continues to hold its place as a reliable, heritage line within the Oliva portfolio, offering a familiar experience that reflects the company’s roots.

description: Partagas is one of the oldest cigar brands in the world, tracing its roots back to 1845 in Havana. For most of that history, the brand built its reputation on Cameroon leaf, which became so closely associated with the Partagas name that it is essentially synonymous with it. The Valle Verde keeps that tradition intact by using a vintage 2015 Cameroon as the binder, but breaks new ground everywhere else. Named for the Green Valley growing region in the state of Veracruz, Mexico, this cigar marks the first time a Partagas has ever been released with a Mexican San Andrés wrapper. The Valle Verde region sits at elevation along the Gulf of Mexico, where a cooler climate and volcanic soil produce a lighter-colored San Andrés leaf that behaves differently than the dark, heavy wrappers the variety is typically known for. The filler rounds out the blend with additional Mexican San Andrés alongside Nicaraguan tobaccos from Jalapa and Estelí, all from the 2020 harvest. Rolled at the HATSA factory in Danlí, Honduras, it is a medium-bodied cigar that earns its place in the Partagas lineup.

Los Gloriosos brings his signature touch back into the fold with an Ecuadorian Habano wrapper and a Nicaraguan/ Dominican blend underneath. Medium to full-bodied with a steady mix of earth, espresso, and a little pepper, it’s balanced and bold.  A welcome return, and one that earns its place in any humidor.
